codeReview.summary: The fix addresses the rerun blocker by translating fixture older-history requests from the dashboard global offset contract into appended-history-relative slicing. The updated tests cover the previously blocked starts at the stored-match total and the next offset, and I did not find blocking or non-blocking issues in the scoped implementation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker transcript again shows a guaranteed failed attempt to execute .factory/init.sh on Windows before following the documented no-op fallback. The mission AGENTS guidance already says not to run the POSIX script directly on this host, but the worker-base startup procedure still appears to prompt the attempt.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for fdab3f34-1277-4ab4-a2fb-d7d299eb75c5 includes an Execute call that tries to run C:\Users\bendc\Documents\GitHub\Leagueback-WEBAPP\.factory\init.sh via Python; the handoff skillFeedback marks this as a deviation and suggests a Windows-safe skip. AGENTS.md Testing & Validation Guidance says not to attempt .factory/init.sh directly on this Windows host unless a compatible POSIX shell is available.

summary: Pass: commit aa431f944b739a4a167e50b26e5ce670423141fe fixes the fixture offset bridge so stored-history exhaustion now lines up with the append-history runtime contract that VAL-CROSS-003 needed.
